title: Medical devices with precise distal steering expandable distal member
abstract: According to some embodiments, an intraluminal medical device comprises a first member, a portion of the first member at, along or near the distal end comprising at least one feature, portion or member having a spiral or helical shape, wherein the distal end of the first member comprises a preformed portion that is angled or bent relative to a longitudinal axis of the device, a second member, the first member comprising a receiving area configured to receive the at least one feature, portion or member having a spiral or helical shape of the first member when the first member is moved relative to the second member along the longitudinal axis, wherein when the at least one feature, portion or member having a spiral or helical shape of the first member is moved at least partially within the receiving area of the second member, the distal end of the first member rotates or turns along the longitudinal axis.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An intraluminal medical device comprising: a first member having a distal end and a longitudinal axis, a portion of the first member at, along or near the distal end comprising at least one feature, portion or member having a spiral or helical shape; wherein the distal end of the first member comprises a preformed portion that is angled or bent relative to the longitudinal axis; a second member coaxially aligned with the first member, the first member comprising a receiving area configured to receive the at least one feature, portion or member having a spiral or helical shape of the first member when the first member is moved relative to the second member along the longitudinal axis; wherein when the at least one feature, portion or member having a spiral or helical shape of the first member is moved at least partially within the receiving area of the second member, the distal end of the first member rotates or turns along the longitudinal axis; a third member coaxially aligned with the first and second members, the third member configured to be removably positioned within an interior of the preformed portion of the first member, wherein movement of the third member relative to the interior of the preformed portion changes an angle of the preformed portion relative to the longitudinal axis; and a handle configured to be grasped by a user of the device. 2. The device of Claim 1, wherein the handle comprises at least one controller. 3. The device of Claim 2, wherein the at least one controller is configured to control a movement of the at least one feature, portion or member having a spiral or helical shape of the first member relative to the receiving area of the second member to rotate the distal end of the first member about the longitudinal axis. 4. The device of Claim 2, wherein the at least one controller is configured to control a movement of the third member relative to the interior of the interior of the preformed portion to bend or change the angle of the preformed portion relative to the longitudinal axis. 5. The device of Claim 2, wherein the at least one controller is positioned on, in or along the handle. 6. The device of Claim 1, wherein the preformed portion is curved or nonlinear. 7. The device of Claim 1, wherein the third member is configured to pass through an interior of the at least one feature, portion or member having a spiral or helical shape. 8. The device of Claim 1, wherein the at least one feature, portion or member having a spiral or helical shape is located along the distal 0 to 20 percent of the first member. 9. The device of Claim 1, wherein the device comprises at least one of a guidewire, a microcatheter, a catheter and another intraluminal device. 10. The device of Claim 1, wherein the second member comprises a microcatheter, a catheter or another intraluminal device. 11. The device of Claim 1, wherein a spiral or helical portion of the at least one feature, portion or member having a spiral or helical shape is angled 1 to 80 degrees relative to the longitudinal axis. 12. The device of Claim 1, wherein at least one of the first member, the second member and the third member is removable relative to another of the first member, the second member and the third member. 13. The device of Claim 1, wherein the first member comprises at least one of nickel titanium (Nitinol), another shape memory' material, another metal or alloy and a polymer. 14. The device of Claim 1. wherein the at least one feature, portion or member having a spiral or helical shape comprises at least one of nickel titanium (Nitinol), another shape memoiv material, another metal or alloy and a polymer. 15. The device of Claim 1, wherein the first member comprises or is coupled to an elongate portion or member that extends proximal to the at least one feature, portion or member having a spiral or helical shape. 16. The device of Claim 15, wherein the elongate portion or member comprises a hypotube. 17. The device of Claim 15, wherein the elongate portion or member comprises at least one of a thermoplastic and a metal or alloy. 18. The device of Claim 1, wherein the device comprises a radially expandable portion configured to be moved between a radially contracted orientation and a radially expanded position. 19. The device of Claim 18. wherein the radially expandable portion is configured to be moved between the radially contracted and radially expanded positions using a fluid-based inflation member or feature. 20. The device of Claim 18, wherein the radially expandable portion is configured to be moved between the radially contracted and radially expanded positions using a mechanical expansion member or feature. 21. A method of advancing an intraluminal device relative to an anatomical luminal system of a subject, the method comprising: positioning a device according to Claim 1 within the anatomical luminal system of the subj ect; advancing the device through the anatomical luminal system of the subject by selectively rotating and bending the distal end of the device; wherein rotating the distal end of the device comprises manipulating a first controller of the at least controller, wherein manipulating the first controller moves the at least one feature, portion or member having a spiral or helical shape of the first member relative to the receiving area of the second member; and wherein bending the distal end of the device comprises manipulating a second controller of the at least controller, wherein manipulating the second controller moves the third member relative to the interior of the preformed portion of the first member; and removing the device from the subject. 22. The method of Claim 21, wherein the anatomical luminal system of the subject comprises a vascular system. 23. A device comprising: a first member having a distal end and a longitudinal axis, a portion of the first member at, along or near the distal end comprising at least one feature having a spiral or helical shape; wherein a length of the at least one feature is configured to change along the longitudinal axis; 

 a second member configured to be moved relative to the first member, wherein linear movement along the longitudinal axis of the second member relative to the first member is configured to impart a force along and change the length of the at least one feature of the first member, wherein a change in the length of the at least one feature is configured to rotate the first member about the longitudinal axis. a third member with a longitudinal axis having a proximal end and a distal end configured to be moved relative to the first member, wherein linear movement along the longitudinal axis of the third member relative to the first member is configured to change the angle of deflection of the first member relative to the longitudinal axis. 24. The device of Claim 23, wherein the outer diameter of the portion comprising the at least one feature is equal to or greater than the inner diameter of the second member. 25. The device of Claim 23, wherein rotation of the first member about the longitudinal axis is independent of the change of the angle of deflection of the first member about the longitudinal axis. 26. The device of Claim 23, wherein the portion comprising the at least one feature is located along the distal 0 to 20 percent of the first member. 27. The device of Claim 23, wherein the device comprises at least one of a guidewire, a microcatheter or other catheter and another intraluminal device. 28. The device of Claim 23, wherein the at least one feature comprises a helical angle of 1 to 80 degrees relative to the longitudinal axis. 29. The device of Claim 23, wherein a third member configured to be moved relative to the first member, wherein linear movement along the longitudinal axis of the third member relative to the first member is configured to impart a force along and change the length of the at least one feature of the first member, wherein a change in the length of the at least one feature is configured to decrease the outer diameter of the portion comprising the at least one feature of the first member. 30. The device of Claim 23, wherein the first member is at least partially removable relative to the second member. 31. The device of Claim 23, wherein the first member comprises at least one of nickel titanium (Nitinol), another shape memory material, another metal or alloy and a polymer. 32. A device comprising: a first member having a distal end and a longitudinal axis, a portion of the first member at, along or near the distal end comprising at least one feature having a spiral or helical shape and a portion of the first member at, along or near the distal end comprising at least one feature configured to undergo radial expansion; wherein a length of the at least one feature having a spiral or helical shape and a length of the at least one feature configured to undergo radial expansion are configured to change along the longitudinal axis; a second member configured to be moved relative to the first member, wherein linear movement along the longitudinal axis of the second member relative to the first member is configured to change the length of the at least one feature having a spiral or helical shape of the first member and cause the first member to rotate about the longitudinal axis; and a third member with a longitudinal axis having a proximal end and a distal end configured to be moved relative to the first member, wherein linear movement along the longitudinal axis of the third member relative to the first member is configured to change the angle of deflection of the first member relative to the longitudinal axis. 33. The device of Claim 32, wherein a third member configured to be moved relative to the first member, wherein linear movement along the longitudinal axis of the third member relative to the first member is configured to impart a force along and change the length of the at least one feature having a spiral or helical shape of the first member, wherein a change in the length of the at least one feature is configured to decrease the outer diameter of the portion comprising the at least one feature of the first member. 34. The device of Claim 32, wherein a third member configured to be moved relative to the first member, wherein proximal displacement along the longitudinal axis of the third member relative to the first member is configured to impart a force along and change the length of the at least one portion configured to undergo radial expansion, wherein a change in the length of the at least one portion configured to undergo radial expansion is configured to increase the outer diameter of the at least one portion configured to undergo radial expansion. 35. The device of Claim 32, wherein the first member comprises at least one of nickel titanium (Nitinol), another shape memory material, another metal or alloy and polymer. 36. The device of Claim 32. wherein the device comprises at least one of a guidewire, a microcatheter or other catheter and another intraluminal device. 37. The device of Claim 32, wherein the first member is at least partially removable relative to the second member. 38. The device of Claim 32, wherein a pitch or spacing of the at least one feature can vary along the portion of the first member. 39. The device of Claim 32, wherein the first member is positioned within an interior of the second member. 40. The device of Claim 32, wherein the third member is positioned within an interior of the second member. 41. The device of Claim 32, wherein the at least one feature comprises a helical angle of 1 to 80 degrees relative to the longitudinal axis.
